title: Shift constraint enforcement from advisory memory to automated harness guards
context: Hunch tracked decisions and bugs, but constraints were zero-count and unenforced. Critical invariants (e.g. 'vectors are derived, never source of truth') existed only in docs/memory and drifted without tooling to prevent violations.
decision: Architects record invariants via `hunch record-constraint`, which the Claude Code agent hook enforces before edits. Firmness levels (off/advisory/firm/strict) let teams tune enforcement strength. The hook injects relevant constraints into agent context and can deny edits hitting blocking invariants, but never breaks flow (exits 0 on any error).
consequences: Load-bearing invariants become mechanically unbreakable at 'strict' firmness — not advisory-only.
Enforcement is team-wide via one committed knob (`hunch firmness`) — shared rules, not per-developer.
Hook grounds the agent in real-time before edits, preventing violations early rather than catching them in CI.
Blocking invariants require explicit human recording — teams must articulate non-negotiable rules.
alternatives_rejected: Keep constraints advisory-only in CLAUDE.md — relies on LLM recall, decays over time.
Make all constraints blocking by default — too rigid; not all invariants are equally critical.
Enforce constraints via CI linting alone — reactive, not proactive; misses the edit stage where prevention is cheap.
Per-developer or per-project firmness — defeats shared enforcement; team needs one agreed level.
